summary: DHH documents Basecamp's second major outage in a week, caused by a sustained cloud storage provider failure that took Basecamp 3 mostly offline for about two hours. He expresses genuine shame and frustration, acknowledging that the repeated failures have exposed deep organizational unpreparedness despite their confidence otherwise. The company is halting all major product development to address single points of failure and pulling back from their cloud migration until they can build a multi-region, multi-provider setup. The post includes a detailed play-by-play timeline of the incident and recovery.
key_points: Basecamp suffered its second major outage in one week due to a cloud storage provider failure, dropping from aspirations of 99.999% uptime to struggling for 99%
DHH acknowledges the outages have called into question Basecamp's entire risk management and operational structure
All major product development is being halted to focus on eliminating single points of failure
Basecamp is pulling back from cloud migration until they can commit to a multi-region, multi-provider architecture
The post serves as both a public apology and a transparent incident report with timestamped updates
money_quotes: This goes much deeper than just a few lessons. It has called into question our entire risk management and operational structure at Basecamp.
From reaching for 99.999% in uptime – the hallowed five nines! – we're now scrambling for two of them. From riches of reliance to rags of shambles.
I'm not going to bother you with platitudes about 'lessons to be learnt', because I've already done that just a few days ago.
We're stopping all major product development at Basecamp for the moment, and dedicating all our attention to fixing these single points of failure that the recent cloud outages have revealed.
To say this is humbling is an epic understatement.

That's incredibly painful. Both because it's because we're failing our customers for the second time in a week, but also because it's showing us just how unprepared we've been as an organization to deal with these cloud challenges, despite our belief otherwise.
key_insight: Repeated cloud outages forced Basecamp to confront that their risk management was fundamentally inadequate, leading them to halt product development and rethink their entire cloud migration strategy.
